title: Savory Middle Eastern Taleggio Kibbeh
description: A briny and creamy Middle Eastern dish featuring taleggio cheese, served as kibbeh to cure hangovers, with a soffritto base and low glycemic index friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion until translucent.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, cooked onion and garlic, bulgur, spices, salt, and pepper, and mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Crumble the softened taleggio cheese into the bowl and mix until the cheese is evenly distributed throughout the meat mixture.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small football shapes, about 1 1/2 inches long and 1 inch wide.
Place the kibbeh on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each kibbeh.
Bake the kibbeh in the preheated oven for 18 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly browned on the bottom.
Serve the kibbeh hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
